An organisational unit has a type that allows a fine-grained structuring of the company structure.
The UnitId is the unique identifier of an organisational unit. The ID is unique for each structure and cannot be changed.

Synchronization in the user administration can be activated on an organisational unit. If synchronization has been activated, the structure is transferred 1:1 to the user administration. Manual changes in the user administration are then no longer permitted and every change in the organization is transferred to the user administration. Please note that all subordinate organisational units are synchronized when activated on an organisational unit.
If synchronization has been activated on an organisational unit, the linked user category is displayed here.
The entity has attributes on a time axis. This list shows all available time axis.
The name of the organisational unit. This field is on the time axis. The number of entries must match the number of time axes of the TimeAxis field.

The org code is used to uniquely identify an organizational unit. In contrast to the unit ID, this is a more readable variant. The ID is unique for each structure and cannot be changed. This field is on the time axis. The number of entries must match the number of time axes of the TimeAxis field.

The org nr is used to uniquely identify an organizational unit. In contrast to the unit ID, this is a more readable variant. The ID is unique for each structure and cannot be changed. This field is on the time axis. The number of entries must match the number of time axes of the TimeAxis field.
The address of the organizational unit. This field is on the time axis. The number of entries must match the number of time axes of the TimeAxis field.
The level at which the organizational unit is affiliated. This field is on the time axis. The number of entries must match the number of time axes of the TimeAxis field.
The organisational unit is the basic element for the hierarchical structuring of the company.
